perturbation.original_text: In the event that Receiving Party becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Confidential Information, Receiving Party shall provide MyPoints.com with prompt notice so that it may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. In the event MyPoints.com is unable to obtain such protective order or other appropriate remedy, only that portion of the Confidential Information which has been deemed by a written opinion of counsel to be legally required to be furnished, shall be disclosed, and Receiving Party will cooperate with the MyPoints.com to obtain a protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded the Confidential Information so disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that Receiving Party believes it is legally compelled to disclose any of the Confidential Information, Receiving Party will attempt to provide MyPoints.com with notice so that it may seek a protective order or other remedy. In the event MyPoints.com is unable to obtain such protective order or other remedy, that portion of the Confidential Information which it subjectively believes is legally required to be furnished, may be disclosed, and Receiving Party will consider cooperating with the MyPoints.com.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'is legally compelled' to 'believes it is legally compelled,' from 'shall provide' to 'will attempt to provide,' and from 'shall be disclosed' to 'may be disclosed' significantly weakens the obligation to protect confidential information. The subjective 'believes' and 'may' allows the Receiving Party to disclose information without strict legal justification, and limits their accountability. Removing the need for a written opinion of counsel, makes it even more ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Illinois Rules of Professional Conduct, Rule 1.6
perturbation.law_citation: Illinois Rules of Professional Conduct, Rule 1.6
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification potentially encourages a violation of Illinois Rule of Professional Conduct 1.6, which governs confidentiality of information. Rule 1.6(b)(6) allows disclosure only to comply with other law or court order. The modified language encourages the Receiving Party to use their belief about legal requirements, making it more prone to errors of legal judgement. This potentially forces Mypoints.com to violate Rule 1.6
perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates specific actions to protect confidential information, while the modified version weakens these obligations, creating a legal ambiguity. The original states 'Receiving Party shall provide,' while the changed text says 'will attempt to provide.' The original says 'shall be disclosed,' while the changed text states 'may be disclosed.' Furthermore, the change removes the need for 'a written opinion of counsel,' making it even more ambiguous. This ambiguity undermines the enforceability of the confidentiality provisions, which creates a legal contradiction, as it makes it difficult to determine what actions the Receiving Party is actually obligated to take. The perturbation explanation highlights the weakening of the obligation to protect confidential information by introducing subjective elements. Without any law snippets, it is difficult to point to which law this contradiction violates; however, the change in language weakens the obligation creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party warrants and represents that it possesses all necessary power, right and authority to lawfully execute and perform the obligations set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party generally believes that it possesses the power, right and authority to execute and perform most of the obligations set forth herein.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'warrants and represents' to 'generally believes' and the addition of 'most of' introduces significant ambiguity. A warranty is a guarantee, while a belief is merely an opinion. Also, stating 'most of the obligations' introduces ambiguity as to which obligations the Receiving Party might not be able to perform, potentially undermining the entire agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Illinois Contract Law - Formation of Contract
perturbation.law_explanation: Under Illinois contract law, a contract requires a clear manifestation of mutual assent and a meeting of the minds, which is an intention to be bound. The modified clause undermines the intent to be bound by creating doubt about the Receiving Party's ability to fulfill key obligations. This makes the agreement potentially unenforceable due to lack of certainty and consideration.

perturbation.justification: The original text provides a warranty and representation that the Receiving Party possesses all necessary power, right, and authority to lawfully execute and perform the obligations set forth in the contract. The changed text weakens this by stating the Receiving Party 'generally believes' it possesses the power and authority to perform 'most of' the obligations. This introduces ambiguity and undermines the certainty required for a valid contract under Illinois law, as explained in the Law Explanation, because it creates doubt about the Receiving Party's ability to fulfill key obligations. Although no specific law snippet is provided, the Law Explanation states that Illinois law requires a clear manifestation of mutual assent and an intention to be bound, which is undermined by the ambiguous language in the changed text. The shift from a warranty to a belief weakens the assurance of performance, potentially rendering the agreement unenforceable.
